source.module: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

topicTags: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

content.problemStatement:
In Django templating, filters and comments help manipulate data and add notes to your templates.

**Filters:** \
Filters in Django templates allow you to modify variables before they are displayed. They are applied using the pipe `|` symbol after a variable. Here are some common filters:

1. **lowercase**: Converts text to lowercase \
   `{{ name|lowercase }}`

2. **upper**: Converts text to uppercase \
   `{{ name|upper }}`

3. **title**: Capitalizes the first letter of each word \
   `{{ title|title }}`

4. **length**: Returns the length of a list or string \
   `{{ my_list|length }}`

5. **date**: Formats a date according to the given format \
   `{{ today|date:"Y-m-d" }}`

You can chain multiple filters:
`{{ name|lower|capfirst }}`

**Comments:** \
Comments in Django templates allow you to add notes or temporarily disable parts of your template. \
There are two types of comments:

1. **Single-line comments:**
   `{# This is a single-line comment #}`

2. **Multi-line comments:** \
   `{% comment %}` \
   `This is a` \
   `multi-line comment` \
   `{% endcomment %}`

Comments are not rendered in the final HTML output, making them useful for developers without affecting the user experience.
